label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So, Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. The question is asking what the date would be 24 hours later, and we need to express it in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe I can check each one. First, let me make sure I understand the starting date. The problem says "tomorrow" is July 29, 2002. That means today's date, relative to when Jane booked the flight, must be July 28, 2002. But we're focusing on the flight date, which is tomorrow (July 29). Now, adding 24 hours to that date. Wait, adding 24 hours to a date usually means moving to the next day. For example, if today is July 29, then 24 hours later would be July 30. So if the flight is on July 29, then 24 hours after that would be July 30. Let me confirm that. Let me think of a specific time. Suppose the flight is on July 29 at, say, 10 AM. Then 24 hours later would be July 30 at 10 AM. So regardless of the time of day, adding exactly 24 hours moves to the next day. So the date would be July 30, 2002. Now looking at the options. The options are: (A) 07/29/2001 – that's a year earlier, so no. (B) 04/24/2002 – April? Doesn't seem related. (C) 07/30/2002 – this matches my calculation, July 30th. (D) 07/28/2002 – that's the day before the flight, so subtracting 24 hours, not adding. (E) 07/29/2002 – same date, but we added 24 hours, so this would only be correct if we stayed on the same date but 24 hours later, which doesn't
